A URL.
Handle scheduled update tasks in the background, and respond to background system interactions including Siri intents and incoming Bluetooth messages.
Create an extended runtime session that continues running your app after the user stops interacting with it.
An object that defines a portion of an image that you want to call out separately to an assistive app.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether reduced motion is enabled.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether VoiceOver is running.
Tells the interface controller that the reduce motion status has changed.
Tells the interface controller that the VoiceOver status has changed.
An object that encapsulates information about a button displayed in an alert or action sheet.
A block to perform in response to an action.
Constants indicating the style of the action button.
A cancel action. Use this style for an action that cancels the operation without making changes.
The default style. Use this for most of your buttons.
An action that causes destructive behavior to the user’s data or the app. Destructive actions are styled in a way that warns the user of the destructive nature of the action.
Constants indicating the styles for standard system alerts.

An action sheet style. Action sheets are modal sheets that can be dismissed using the Cancel button in the top-left corner of the sheet. You can also add one or two custom buttons to perform related tasks.
An alert sheet with stacked buttons. The alert sheet includes a default Cancel button at the bottom of the sheet. You can add other buttons, which are placed above the Cancel button.
An alert sheet with side-by-side buttons.
The centralized point of control and coordination for apps with a single watchOS app target.
A collection of methods that manages the app-level behavior for a single-target watchOS app.
Creates the application object and the application delegate, and sets up the app’s event cycle.
A task that updates your app’s state in the background.
The running states of the Watch app.
The Watch app is running in the foreground and currently receiving events.
The Watch app is running in the background.
The Watch app is running in the foreground, but is not yet responding to actions from controls or gestures.
An object that stores a reference to an audio file and provides metadata information about that file.
An object that controls playback of a single audio item.
An object that manages the presentation state of an audio file while it is playing.
A notification that the item has played successfully to its end.
A notification that the item failed to play to its end.
Constants that represent the status of a player item.
The item can’t be played.
The item is ready to play.
The item’s status is unknown.
A notification that the item’s current time has changed discontinuously.
Constants that represent the status of the player.
The player can no longer play the audio because of an error. Use the [error](/documentation/watchkit/wkaudiofileplayer/error) property to get information about the error that occurred.
The player is ready to play its item.
The status of the item is unknown because the player has not yet loaded the audio file for playback.
An object that controls playback of one or more audio items.

The title to display on the button that the user taps to accept a recording. The value of this key is an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object. If you do not specify this option, the button title is set to “Save”.

The behavior for showing the action button. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object with a Boolean value. When the value is [true](/documentation/Swift/true), the recording interface always shows the action button. When the value is [false](/documentation/Swift/false), the sheet shows the button only after the user has recorded some audio. The default value for this option is YES.

The automatic recording behavior of the action sheet. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object with a Boolean value. When the value is [true](/documentation/Swift/true), the recording interface starts recording as soon as it is presented. When the value is [false](/documentation/Swift/false), the user must start recording manually. The default value for this option is [true](/documentation/Swift/true).

The maximum length of recorded audio clips. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object with an [TimeInterval](/documentation/Foundation/TimeInterval) value containing the maximum duration in seconds. If you do not specify this option, there is no maximum recording time.
Constants indicating the quality of audio recordings.
A high-quality audio recording. This preset records audio with a 44.1 kHz sampling rate using either the LPCM 705.6 kbps or AAC 96 kbps format.
Audio quality suitable for basic speech recording. This preset records audio with an 8 kHz sampling rate using either the LPCM 128 kbps or AAC 24 kbps format.
Audio quality suitable for higher fidelity speech recording. This preset records audio with a 16 kHz sampling rate using either the LPCM 256 kbps or AAC 32 kbps format.
The result of an attempt to download the content associated with a remote notification.
The download attempt failed.
The download attempt succeeded.
The notification has no associated content.
A task for handling timely Bluetooth alerts in the background.
A collection of methods you can implement to track the user’s interaction with the digital crown, receiving notifications when the user rotates the crown or when rotation stops.
An object that reports the current state of the digital crown, including its rotational speed when it is in motion.
A session that continues to run your app after the user has stopped interacting.
A set of optional methods for monitoring an extended runtime session.

The user has disabled background app refresh.
The session exceeded its resource limits.

The app attempted to start a session, but doesn’t have a valid session type.
The app invalidated the session before it started.
The app attempted to schedule a session too far in the future.
An unknown error occurred.

An error prevented the session from running.
The session used all of its allocated time.
The session ended normally.

Either the session has encountered an error, or it has stopped running.
The app has not yet started or scheduled the session.
The session is actively running.
The app has scheduled the session to run at a future date.

Specifies the directional flow of the user interface.

The layout direction right-to-left. This value is appropriate when your app is running with localizations such as Arabic or Hebrew that should have the user interface layout origin on the right edge of the coordinate system.

An interface element that lets you play video and audio content in your watchOS app.
An object that provides information that is common to all interface objects in your watchOS app.
Constants for horizontally aligning objects in their container.
Center alignment. The object’s content is centered horizontally in the container and extends equally to the left and right.
Left alignment. The object’s content aligns with the left edge of the container and extends to the right.
Right alignment. The object’s content aligns with the right edge of the container and extends to the left.
Constants for vertically aligning objects in their container.
Bottom alignment. The object’s content aligns with the bottom edge of the container and extends upward.
Center alignment. The object’s content is centered vertically in the container and extends equally up and down.
Top alignment. The object’s content aligns with the top edge of the container and extends downward.

A semantic description of the view’s contents, used to determine whether the view should be flipped when switching between left-to-right and right-to-left layouts.
A view that is always displayed using a left-to-right layout.
A view that is always displayed using a left-to-right layout.

A view representing the playback controls, such as Play, Rewind, or Fast Forward buttons or playhead scrubbers. These views are not flipped when switching between left-to-right and right-to-left layouts.

A view representing a directional control, for example a segment control for text alignment, or a D-pad control for a game. These views are not flipped when switching between left-to-right and right-to-left layouts.
The default value for views. The view is flipped when switching between left-to-right and right-to-left layouts.
An interface object that displays a visual separator within a group.
An interface element that lets users select a single floating-point value from a range of values.
An interface element that toggles between an On and Off state.

An interface element that provides control of the audio volume from the watch or a paired iPhone.
The source of the audio managed by the volume control.
Audio playing on iPhone.
Audio playing on Apple Watch.
A gesture recognizer that interprets a touch event that occurs in the same relative area for an extended period of time.

The option to play a media file automatically when it is displayed. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object containing a Boolean value indicating whether the media file should begin playing automatically when the interface is displayed. Playback is also contingent upon the file being available and ready to play on Apple Watch. If you do not specify this option, the user must initiate playback.

The behavior for playing the media in a loop. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object containing a Boolean value. Set the value to [true](/documentation/Swift/true) to play the media file in a loop. If this key is not present, the media file plays one time and then ends.

The number of seconds into the media file at which to begin playback. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object containing an [TimeInterval](/documentation/Foundation/TimeInterval) value. Playback begins at the specified number of seconds past the original start point of the media file. If you do not specify this option, playback begins at the beginning of the media file.

The behavior for resizing the video to fit the available space. The value of this key is an [NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber) object containing an appropriate constant of the [WKVideoGravity](/documentation/watchkit/wkvideogravity) type.
Template images that you can use for menus.
The icon indicating an action to accept an event or item.

The icon indicating an action to decline or cancel an event.

The icon indicating that more actions or options are available.

A gesture recognizer that interprets swiping gestures in one or more directions.

A gesture recognizer that interprets a touch event occurring and ending in approximately the same area on the screen.
Constants that specify a text field’s semantic meaning.
The input modes supported by the text input controller.
Text from dictation and suggestions plus both animated and non animated emoji.
Text from dictation and suggestions plus non animated emoji.
Text from dictation and suggestions only. Do not allow emoji of any kind.

An interface controller object that manages a dynamic user interface for a local or remote notification.
The type of notification interface to display.
A constant indicating that the system should display your dynamic notification interface.
A constant indicating that the system should display the corresponding static interface instead. When you return this value, the system takes responsibility for displaying the notification’s content.
Constants indicating the appearance of video content.
Content is resized to fit the entire bounds rectangle. This option does not preserve the original aspect ratio of the content.
Content is resized to fit the bounds rectangle, preserving the original aspect ratio of the content. Content that does not completely fill the bounds rectangle is centered in the partial axis.
Content is resized to fill the bounds rectangle completely while preserving the original aspect ratio of the content. This option results in cropping of the edges of the video in the axis it exceeds.
A background task used to receive background updates from the Watch Connectivity framework.
Values indicating the water-resistance rating.
A water-resistance rating of IPX7.
A water-resistance rating of 50 meters.
